raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "defensive relativization that normalizes risk in spiritual or metaphysical inquiry by shifting burden onto the critic via rhetorical question",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "no activity or tool used by humans is free of danger",
    "danger alone cannot justify rejecting a practice",
    "the disputed thing is not actually dangerous",
    "universal risk renders selective objection incoherent"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as rhetorical deflection but rests on an unexamined premise of universal danger that weakens under scrutiny of non-risky activities",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the audience must accept that all human endeavors carry comparable metaphysical or existential risk and that open exploration outweighs caution",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "some practices or tools genuinely lack comparable danger while the statement is used to equate them with high-risk ones, thereby discouraging legitimate discernment"
}
